channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Kamala Harris leaves door open for 2028 presidential run
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kamala Harris isn’t ruling out another run for the White House. In an interview with the BBC posted Saturday, Harris said she expects a woman will be president in the coming years, and it could “possibly” be her. “I am not done,” she said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Potential 2028 contender campaigns for Spanberger in key Tidewater area
item.description: Pennsylvania governor Josh Shapiro campaigns for Virginia's Abigail Spanberger as 2028 presidential speculation grows around the Democratic rising star.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A report claims left-wing terrorism is rising. The data paints a complicated picture
item.description: The report's claim comes with caveats. Its critics say it does more to reveal issues around collecting and analyzing domestic terrorism data than it does to clarify the current state of the problem.
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef, MPR News

item.title: AG James' 'horrible judgment' backing Mamdani puts New Yorkers in 'jeopardy': GOP challenger
item.description: GOP challenger Michael Henry slammed NY attorney general Letitia James over her endorsement of socialist mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in a Fox News Digital interview.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Schumer accuses Trump of 'skipping town' during shutdown as president heads to Asia for trade talks
item.description: Sen. Chuck Schumer blasts President Donald Trump for leaving the United States for Asia during the government shutdown, accusing the president of "skipping town.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Malaysia warns ASEAN neutrality eroding as global powers compete
item.description: Malaysian Foreign Minister Mohamad Hasan has warned Southeast Asian counterparts that neutrality is shrinking due to big power rivalry.
item.dc:creator: Eileen Ng, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Reagan Foundation becomes the latest US institution drawn into Donald Trump's controversies
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation and Institute is drawing a volatile mix of blowback and praise for arguing that a Canadian government advertisement playing in U.S. markets misrepresented the 40th president’s words to blast President Donald Trump’s tariff policies.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon accepts $130 million donation to help pay the military during the government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon confirmed Friday that it has accepted an anonymous $130 million gift to help pay members of the military during the government shutdown, raising ethical questions after President Donald Trump had announced that a friend had offered the gift to defray any shortfalls.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York Attorney General Letitia James pleads not guilty in mortgage fraud case pushed by Trump
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — New York Attorney General Letitia James accused the Trump administration of using the justice system as a “tool of revenge” after she pleaded not guilty Friday in a federal mortgage fraud case the president pressed the Justice Department to bring.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zohran Mamdani responds to ‘racist attacks’ from opponent
item.description: The comments are about much more than just his own identity, but a slight to all Muslim New Yorkers, Mamdani told NewsNation affiliate WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: New York AG Letitia James pleads not guilty to federal fraud charges
item.description: James told a crowd outside the courtroom their support has "strengthened my spirit, and it has anchored my soul.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Poll: California's Prop 50 likely to pass, Republican surges in governor's race
item.description: With less than two weeks before election day, California voters appear poised to pass the state’s redistricting measure, Proposition 50, to counter Republican redistricting efforts in Texas to gain more seats in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: SNAP pause could harm families, say county workers
item.description: Minnesota officials say residents who get food assistance won’t receive their benefits for November, as long as the federal shutdown continues. County workers say they’re now hearing from concerned enrollees in the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Democrats see Hegseth curtailing Congress's oversight role
item.description: Some Democrats are accusing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth of curtailing Congress’s oversight role with his recent order instructing Defense Department personnel to coordinate all interactions with Capitol Hill through the building’s central legislative affairs office.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration uses shutdown to push Education Department elimination
item.description: The Education Department is trying to use the government shutdown to further push for its own elimination.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says friend cut $130M check for military amid shutdown
item.description: The announcement came as a reporter asked Trump about federal workers’ paychecks during the congressional impasse.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: DC police detained man for protesting National Guard patrol with Darth Vader song, lawsuit says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A man who says he was detained by police for following an Ohio National Guard patrol while playing Darth Vader's theme song from “Star Wars” on his phone sued the District of Columbia on Thursday, claiming the officers violated his constitutional rights.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Black enrollment is waning at many elite colleges after affirmative action ban, AP analysis finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After decades of gradual growth, the number of Black students enrolling at many elite colleges has dropped in the two years since the Supreme Court banned affirmative action in admissions, leaving some campuses with Black populations as small as 2% of their freshman class, accordin
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP senator blocks Democratic bill to pay all federal employees during shutdown
item.description: Sen. Ron Johnson (R-Wis.) objected to a request by Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) on Thursday for the Senate to immediately consider and pass a bill to pay all federal workers, members of the military and federal contractors during the 2025 government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump hosts roundtable on admin’s efforts combatting cartels
item.description: Earlier this month, Trump declared the U.S. is now at war with drug cartels deemed by the White House as terrorist groups.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Former Alabama QB AJ McCarron announces run for Lieutenant Governor
item.description: McCarron was with the Tide for five years, winning back-to-back National Championships under coach Nick Saban.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Matteson, NewsNation

item.title: Zohran Mamdani responds to ‘racist attacks’ from opponent
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Democratic nominee for mayor Zohran Mamdani responded on Thursday to his opponent’s controversial remarks about 9/11, calling them “racist attacks.” In an interview with PIX on Politics host Dan Mannarino, Mamdani took an emotional tone, addressing former Gov.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Illinois politician Darren Bailey's son, family killed in crash
item.description: Republican candidate for Illinois governor Darren Bailey lost his son, daughter-in-law and two grandchildren in the crash.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Pritzker calls for 'truth, justice' in response to Trump takeover
item.description: "I am angry because this not the country that I want for us," Gov. J.B. Pritzker said while announcing the creation of the Illinois Accountability Commission.
item.dc:creator: Meg Hilling, NewsNation

item.title: Texas book-rating law ruled unconstitutional by federal court for 3rd time
item.description: The two-year long legal battle over a Texas law will draw to an end after a federal court judge ruled Tuesday that the law violates the US Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the final New York City mayor's debate
item.description: There was no shortage of fireworks between Democrat Zohran Mamdani, independent Andrew Cuomo and Republican Curtis Sliwa.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: ICE looking to hire more than 40 health care workers
item.description: As of Wednesday night, there were 48 open job listings in the ICE Health Service Corps on the ICE USA Jobs portal.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Wyoming Capitol evacuated after report of suspected explosive device
item.description: The Wyoming Capitol in Cheyenne was evacuated on Tuesday after a report about a suspected explosive device, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis: Progressive to issue nearly $1 billion in credits to Florida policyholders
item.description: “Thanks to Commissioner (Michael) Yaworsky, he has secured almost a billion dollars in credits for Progressive auto insurance policyholders,” DeSantis said.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USDA reopening 2,100 aid offices despite shutdown
item.description: The Agriculture Department will reopen about 2,100 county offices all across the country Thursday despite the ongoing government shutdown to help farmers and ranchers get access to $3 billion of aid from existing programs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Newsom deploys National Guard to support food banks amid federal shutdown
item.description: (FOX 5/KUSI) -- As the government shutdown reached its 22nd day, California Gov. Gavin Newsom announced on Wednesday he will deploy National Guard troops to support food banks throughout the state.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Anti-science bills hit statehouses, stripping away public health protections built over a century
item.description: More than 420 anti-science bills attacking longstanding public health protections – vaccines, milk safety and fluoride – have been introduced in statehouses across the U.S. this year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate Republicans go to White House in show of unity
item.description: As the government shutdown enters its fourth week, Senate Republicans are headed to the White House — not for urgent talks on how to end it, but for a display of unity with President Donald Trump as they refuse to negotiate on any Democratic demands.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: White House starts demolishing part of the East Wing
item.description: Dramatic photos show construction equipment tearing into the East Wing façade and windows, though the federal agency that oversees such projects has not approved President Trump's 90,000-square-foot, $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: San Diego City Council ordinance limits participation in unauthorized federal operations
item.description: The San Diego City Council unanimously passed an ordinance requiring judicial warrants for federal enforcement to enter City-controlled non-public properties on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: China hits the U.S. back in trade war
item.description: China has expanded its export rules on rare earths, requiring foreign firms to get approval for exporting products containing even small amounts of China-originated materials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: As deadline for Trump’s colleges compact looms, schools signal dissent
item.description: Of the original nine schools that received the Trump administration's Compact for Academic Excellence in Higher Education, the majority have indicated they are not planning on signing.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny, MPR News
